respStmt.resp: Encoded based on the edition by
respStmt.name: Rudolf Kassel (1965)

listWit.witness: manus recentior quae codicem A correxit
translatio latina a Guilelmo de Moerbeke (1278)
translatio arabica ab Abū Bišr (saec. X)
consensus codicis A et codicis Phi

app.note: Lobel notes participio nil opus (cf. De cael. 294a11). Bernays proposed his reading before Arabis 'quae est (sive sunt) sine appellatione' was known.
fort. οὐ χωρὶς, opp. ἀλλὰ κοινῇ line 15 (cf. οὐδὲν δὲ κοινόν line 17)
nil mutandum (vid. Denniston, Greek Part. p. 585)
η super ει scr. m. rec. in A
Holland susp. 'γᾶσ' esse fragmentum nominis Οἰνώπας sive Οίνωνᾶς
fort. αὐτῇ δὲ (ταύτῃ) τῇ (non obstat Ar)
etiam anon. in ed. Oxon. a. 1760
sunt sane inepta, sed ad rationem concludendam necessaria (cf. 1448a29)
omitted at the end of the page in B
corr. recentiores quidam (qui et αὐτοσχεδιαστικὴ pro -ῆς, falso)
cf. Rhein. Mus. 1962, 177 sq.; πρῶτος ἀγωνιστικὸν ex Ar eliciebat Immisch

app.lem: μέγεθος ἐκ μικρῶν μύθων, καὶ ἡ λέξις ἐκ) λέξεως
app.note: decurtata; plura videtur habuisse Sigma, sed omnia turbata in Ar
om. Ar, cuius obscura verba 'ut relinquatur omnis sermo qui est per compendium' ad notam criticam quae fuerit in Sigma incertissima coniectura refert Else
excidit in Lat; non obstat Ar
'dolores' fr Syr, 'passiones et impressiones' Ar; also supported by tract. Coisl. in def. com.
post μέλους lacuna haustam esse καθάρσεως explicationem censent multi
Vahlen corrected the reading found in the manuscripts and the Arabic translation
hic Π: post πραγμάτων (1447a33) transp. Castelvetro

app.rdg: ἐν οἷς οὐκ ἔστι δῆλον ἢ προαιρεῖται ἢ φεύγει

app.note: videntur fuisse interpolata (calamo notavit nescio quis in editionis Oxon. a. 1760 exemplari Bodleiano)
prius καὶ supra αὐτὸ in B
καὶ τὰ τυχόντα συμβαίνει omitted in Ar
et ad quaecumque adhuc et Lat (reflecting καὶ πρὸς τὰ τυχόντα, ἔτι δὲ καὶ?)
ὅλου in ἄλλου corruptum apud Tzetz.
Ar supports Π; irrepsit in vicem τοὺς ἐν τῇ ἀρετῇ, glossema ad τοὺς ἐπιεικεῖς
omitted in Ar; cf. Tkatsch, i. 194
post ἅπερ ἂν in Β καὶ χορηγίας δεόμενον ἐστὶ (ex line 8), tum post unius vel duarum litt. spatium πάθοι τις
post Μήδειαν lacunam statuit explendam (cf. Ar: 'quod non faciat... ubi cognoscunt')
τι incertum in B, prius δι' inductum
post ταῦτα vel post ποιῆσαι (36) lacunam statuit
que sit (... bona, pro χρηστὴν ΑΒ) Lat
προ ex περ corr. in B
τοῦ δὲ ἀνομοίου cum exemplo ante τοῦ δὲ ἀνωμάλου perisse suspicatus est
cf. Porphyrius ap. schol. B Hom. Il. 2. 73
ἡ habuit Σ; Π def. Vahlen
post ἀγαθὸν καὶ transp. Lobel; secl. Ritter, prob. Else qui post καὶ add. ὅμοιον
ἡ Α; ἢ Β; qui Lat
τόξον Β (similia nec minus obscura in Ar); om. Π
ἄλλον δὲ (ἐκεῖνον) vel (αὐτόν), ἄλλον δὲ Sykutris
Spengel secl. καὶ περιδεραίων; fort. delendum (non vertit Ar)
supported by Ar; utique erat (erit Lobel) Lat
secl. Christ (ἔξω τοῦ καθόλου secluserat Duentzer)
τὴν ἀδελφήν ex Ar add. Tkatsch et Gudeman, glossemate abusi
Vahlen olim; malim ὑπό του θεοῦ cum Ellebodio
Gomperz Arabi fisus, cuius testimonium nisi quod λύσις δ᾽ ἡ confirmat incertissimum est
nihil minus (ἧσσον) Lat; ἴσως non vertit Ar
post ἡττηθῇ (23) transp. Susemihl, secl. Gudeman
ante ἀγάθωνος in B; huius Lat O, aut huius Lat
τοῦ ποιητοῦ vertit Ar (?); del. Gudeman, Hardy
supported by Ar and iam Lat
supported by Ar; ἡ ἰδέα Madius (idea Lat)
Bernhardy deleted, Gudeman restored based on context
Kassel notes it should likely be ἢ ἡμιφώνου
ex Ar sic fere emendaveris: οὐ συλλαβή, συλλαβὴ δὲ
corrupta et confusa; 38-3 ἢ... αὑτήν om. Ar, 3-10 ἣν... μέσου om. Β
Tyrwhitt's correction follows the Latin tradition
Ritter follows Ar; cf. De interpr. 16b21 and pap. Theophr. 8
For Arabis 'homo incedit' cf. Tkatsch, i. 193
Diels restored from Ar; Bywater suspected a missing quadruplex noun here
in Ar sequuntur 'dory autem est nobis proprium, populo (?) autem glossa'
G. Valla added 'τὸ πῶν τὸ νᾶπυ τὸ γόνυ τὸ δόρυ τὸ ἄστυ' in the margin
Ar exhibits 'dory poy (?) napy gony', with the fifth word corrupt
Lat reads 'utique' (equivalent to ἂν) followed by a blank space
Vahlen once thought one of these (likely κυρίου) should be deleted
Hermann used rec (-a); in B, there is a hole after the final letter where 'n' may have perished
Lat has a blank space here
Lat O reads 'ipsi'; Lat T reads 'ipsis'
Kassel doubts if this refers to homoeoteleuton
in A, the 'sigma' was erased; Lat has 'bis bis'
Else attributes 'πλέον' and 'καὶ Σίνων καὶ Τρωάδες' to a second interpolator
Hermann thought they were added later, but by Aristotle himself

app.rdg: ταὐτὰ καὶ τὰ τοῦ μύθου μέρη ταὐτά

app.lem: κατά τε τῆς συστάσεως τὸ μῆκος

app.note: Else thought γὰρ καὶ likely correct
in A, was 'μὴ γνύη', final eta in correction
Vahlen corrected the reading found in the manuscripts and the Arabic translation
post ἡδὺ deest Ar usque ad 1461a27
lac. stat. et sic explet: ὀρθῶς, ἥμαρτε δ᾽ ἐν τῷ μιμήσασθαι (sive ἀπεργάσασθαι) δι'
όποιανοῦν (21) et ante et post πεποίηται videtur habuisse Π
que Lat; ει superscr. m. rec.
ἢ inseruit m. rec. in A; nihil Lat
ἡ super μ scr. m. rec. in A
post haec verba rursus praesto est Ar
' m. rec. in A; post ut (ὡς) spat. vac. in Lat
ον induxit m. rec. in B
nomen aliquid Lat; accentus super w in ras. in A
Ar; εἰ in lit., ἐπεὶ m. rec. in A
suppl. Vahlen qui lac. detexit: (καὶ εἰ ἀδύνατον); (καὶ ἴσως ἀδύνατον) cx Ar Gomperz
fort. ἐναντίον (cf. De gen. et corr. 315a23)
autem Lat T, om. Lat O
Gudeman putat hoc nomen in Ar latere
in A, τα αυ scr. m. rec. in loco macula deformato
increpabant Lat; corr. m. rec. in A
aut propriis Lat; in A corr. m. rec.
sequuntur in B περὶ ἰάμβων καὶ κωμῳδίας
